title: Savory French Duck Confit
description: A classic French dish featuring tender duck leg, fresh parsley, and rich flavors, perfect for a romantic dinner. This sugar-free-friendly recipe is sure to impress your date.
instructions: Season the duck legs with salt, black pepper, and thyme.
Heat the duck fat in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Add the sliced shallots to the pot and cook until they're softened and lightly browned.
Add the garlic, bay leaves, and lemon juice to the pot, stirring to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the duck for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Broil the duck legs for 5-7 minutes, or until the skin is crispy and golden brown.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the duck and serve.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
